methods.method_name: Coastal Water Sample Collection and Preparation
methods.description: Process for collecting coastal water samples and preparing them for diatom culture incubation under varying iron concentrations.

steps.description: Coastal waters were collected using a motor-driven boat with a pre-cleaned Niskin sampler and carried to the laboratory inside 20L acid-cleaned polycarbonate carboys.
steps.searchable_summary: collect coastal water samples using Niskin sampler

steps.description: Water samples were filtered with GF/F filters to remove particulate biomass, followed by filtration using 0.2µm polycarbonate filters to remove bacterial biomass.
steps.searchable_summary: filter water samples to remove biomass

steps.description: Initial nutrient levels were measured and adjusted to specified levels. Diatom cultures were incubated under a range of Fe concentrations with a control kept without Fe addition.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust nutrient levels and incubate diatom cultures

steps.description: Fe was chelated with EDTA and added to reach target concentrations. Pre-cultures were added to experimental bottles to achieve a final Chl a concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: chelate Fe with EDTA and add pre-cultures
outputs: experimental bottles with target Fe concentration

steps.description: Bottles were mixed gently and transported to an outdoor incubator. Cultures were grown under natural conditions and mixed daily to prevent sedimentation.

methods.method_name: Chlorophyll a and Biogenic Silica Analysis
methods.description: Procedures for analyzing chlorophyll a and biogenic silica in water samples to assess growth rates and silica content.

steps.description: 100 ml samples were filtered using GF/F filter for total Chl a estimation and kept at -20ºC until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: filter samples for Chl a estimation

steps.description: Filters were soaked in DMF and fluorescence was measured using a spectrophotometer.
100 ml aliquots were filtered for biogenic silica analysis, washed, dried, and digested in NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples for biogenic silica analysis

steps.description: Silicate concentration was measured using colorimetric methodology.

methods.description: High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) method for quantifying pigments in water samples.

steps.description: Water samples were filtered on GF/F filters using a vacuum pump under low light and stored at -20ºC.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and store samples for HPLC analysis

steps.description: Filters were soaked in acetone and cells were broken using a tissue homogenizer followed by cold centrifugation.
steps.searchable_summary: extract pigments using acetone and homogenization

steps.description: Pigments were quantified using HPLC with a C8 column and a methanol-based solvent system.
